comments.body:
_🛠️ Refactor suggestion_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Extract the vendor status filter to eliminate duplication.**

The vendor status filter condition (lines 34-38 and 43-47) is duplicated with only a negation difference. If the filter criteria change (e.g., adding another vendor or refining the backtick check), both locations must be updated identically, which is error-prone.




<details>
<summary>♻️ Proposed refactor using jq `def`</summary>

```diff
 jq '
+  def is_vendor_status:
+    (.kind == "unknown")
+    and (.authorLogin == "qodo-code-review" or .authorLogin == "qodo-merge" or .authorLogin == "coderabbitai")
+    and ((.body // "") | contains("```") | not);
+
   .vendors as $vendors
   | (.comments // []) as $comments
-  | ($comments
-      | map(
-          select(
-            (.kind == "unknown")
-            and (.authorLogin == "qodo-code-review" or .authorLogin == "qodo-merge" or .authorLogin == "coderabbitai")
-            and ((.body // "") | contains("```") | not)
-          )
-        )) as $vendor_statuses
-  | ($comments
-      | map(
-          select(
-            (
-              (.kind == "unknown")
-              and (.authorLogin == "qodo-code-review" or .authorLogin == "qodo-merge" or .authorLogin == "coderabbitai")
-              and ((.body // "") | contains("```") | not)
-            )
-            | not
-          )
-        )) as $relevant_comments
+  | ($comments | map(select(is_vendor_status))) as $vendor_statuses
+  | ($comments | map(select(is_vendor_status | not))) as $relevant_comments
   | ($relevant_comments | map(select((.kind == "finding") and (.is_outdated != true) and (.is_resolved != true)))) as $findings
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In @.agents/skills/pr-review/scripts/triage_pr_review.sh around lines 32 - 55,
Introduce a jq helper def named is_vendor_status that encapsulates the vendor
filter (checks .authorLogin against the vendor logins and the body backtick
check) and then replace the duplicated select(...) expressions with
map(select(is_vendor_status)) for vendor_statuses and
map(select(is_vendor_status | not)) for relevant_comments; update references in
the pipeline where vendor_statuses and relevant_comments are defined (keep the
names vendor_statuses, relevant_comments, comments, findings) so the rest of the
pipeline is unchanged.
```

</details>

<!-- walkthrough_start -->

## Walkthrough

This PR implements a billing noise filter for the PR review triage system. The triage shell script now separates vendor-authored unknown-kind comments (from Qodo and Coderabbit without code fences) into a distinct vendor status set, excludes them from relevant findings, and computes outcomes based on non-vendor comments only. The JSON output now includes `vendor_status_count` for both needs_patch and clean outcomes. Tests validate that Qodo billing-limit comments produce clean outcomes with vendor counts, while actionable code-review comments produce needs_patch outcomes. Documentation explains the feature's rationale and test strategy.

<!-- walkthrough_end -->

🧹 Nitpick comments (1)
docs/product/delivery/phase-09/ticket-01-billing-noise-filter.md (1)
`46-50`: _⚡ Quick win_ **Clarify the Red section inconsistency inline.** Line 50 notes that the Red section's instruction to "assert `needs_patch: true`" was intentionally changed to assert `clean` instead, which makes sense for a proper failing test. However, line 17 still contains the original incorrect instruction. Consider either updating line 17 to reflect what was actually done, or adding an inline note in the Red section pointing forward to the Rationale for the corrected approach.
📝 Proposed clarification Option 1: Update line 17 directly: ```diff -- Run `triage_pr_review.sh` against the fixture and assert the triage output contains `needs_patch: true` — this is the bug we are fixing; the test must fail before the filter exists. +- Run `triage_pr_review.sh` against the fixture and assert the triage output contains `outcome: "clean"` — since the filter doesn't exist yet, the comment will escalate and the outcome will be `needs_patch`, causing this assertion to fail (red state). ``` Option 2: Add a note referencing the Rationale: ```diff -- Run `triage_pr_review.sh` against the fixture and assert the triage output contains `needs_patch: true` — this is the bug we are fixing; the test must fail before the filter exists. +- Run `triage_pr_review.sh` against the fixture and assert the triage output contains `needs_patch: true` — this is the bug we are fixing; the test must fail before the filter exists. *(Note: see Rationale for the corrected test approach that was actually implemented.)* ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. In `@docs/product/delivery/phase-09/ticket-01-billing-noise-filter.md` around lines 46 - 50, The Red section claims the test asserted needs_patch:true but you actually changed the assertion to clean to create a proper failing test; update the earlier occurrence that still states the old instruction (the earlier paragraph in this doc) to match the implemented behavior, or add a short inline note in the Red section pointing readers to the Rationale (or the p9-01.test.ts regression description) explaining that the assertion was intentionally changed from needs_patch to clean so the test fails before the filter exists. ```
🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents ``` Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. Inline comments: In @.agents/skills/pr-review/scripts/triage_pr_review.sh: - Around line 32-55: Introduce a jq helper def named is_vendor_status that encapsulates the vendor filter (checks .authorLogin against the vendor logins and the body backtick check) and then replace the duplicated select(...) expressions with map(select(is_vendor_status)) for vendor_statuses and map(select(is_vendor_status | not)) for relevant_comments; update references in the pipeline where vendor_statuses and relevant_comments are defined (keep the names vendor_statuses, relevant_comments, comments, findings) so the rest of the pipeline is unchanged. --- Nitpick comments: In `@docs/product/delivery/phase-09/ticket-01-billing-noise-filter.md`: - Around line 46-50: The Red section claims the test asserted needs_patch:true but you actually changed the assertion to clean to create a proper failing test; update the earlier occurrence that still states the old instruction (the earlier paragraph in this doc) to match the implemented behavior, or add a short inline note in the Red section pointing readers to the Rationale (or the p9-01.test.ts regression description) explaining that the assertion was intentionally changed from needs_patch to clean so the test fails before the filter exists. ```
